top_weaknesses.issue: Aphorism/abstract-noun stack ('Real numbers, wrong moat,' 'sharecropping with extra steps,' 'embedding-grade fidelity,' 'layer-by-layer') reads as over-polished and pre-owned in places
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut 2-3 aphorisms; replace one with a concrete portability-failure example (a real export attempt, a specific missing field)

top_weaknesses.issue: Voice flips at the policy outro ('Mandated portability with embedding-grade fidelity. Antitrust treatment of the GPU and cloud bottlenecks...') — drifts from operator-critic to think-tank memo
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut or sharpen the policy paragraph; either drop entirely or condense to one sentence in the same voice as the export-button line

top_weaknesses.issue: $2,000 workstation / 70B claim is hand-wavy for HN readers who will immediately argue RAM, quantization, latency, quality
top_weaknesses.fix: Either name the specific config (model + quant + tokens/sec) or downgrade to 'increasingly feasible' with a footnote

top_weaknesses.issue: Anchor text could name sources more explicitly ('around $78 million' could include 'Epoch AI estimate of GPT-4 training cost')
top_weaknesses.fix: Add source/concept name to two anchor texts

consensus_summary: Sharp thesis with a memorable closing punch ('Whoever owns the context owns the user'); H2s outline a clean arc and the export-button passage is the strongest. Held back by aphorism density, a hand-wavy local-AI claim, and a think-tank policy outro that breaks voice.
